sentences.english: Welcome back to "Daily Growth," the podcast where we talk about small habits that make a big difference.
Today's topic: learning a second language.
sentences.korean: 작은 습관이 큰 차이를 만드는 팟캐스트죠. 오늘 주제는 제2외국어 학습입니다.

sentences.english: Now, I know what some of you are thinking — "I tried learning Spanish in high school and I was terrible at it." But here's the thing: the way languages are taught in most schools is not the way our brains actually learn languages.
sentences.korean: 여러분 중 일부가 생각하시는 거 알아요 — '고등학교 때 스페인어 배우려 했는데 정말 못했어.'

sentences.english: The biggest mistake people make is focusing too much on grammar rules and vocabulary lists.
sentences.korean: 사람들이 가장 많이 하는 실수는 문법 규칙과 단어 목록에 너무 집중하는 거예요.

sentences.english: That's like trying to learn to swim by reading a textbook about swimming.

quiz.question: What does '골디락스 존 (적당한 수준)' mean in English?

sentences.english: You have to get in the water.

Research in language acquisition shows that the single most important factor in learning a language is comprehensible input — that means listening to and reading content that you can mostly understand, with just a few new words mixed in.
sentences.korean: 언어 습득 연구에 따르면 언어를 배우는 데 가장 중요한 요소는 이해 가능한 입력이에요.

sentences.english: Not too easy, not too hard.
Think of it as the Goldilocks zone of language learning.
Start by finding content you actually enjoy — podcasts, YouTube channels, TV shows — in your target language.
sentences.korean: 실제로 즐기는 콘텐츠를 찾는 것부터 시작하세요 — 배우려는 언어로 된 팟캐스트, 유튜브, TV쇼요.

sentences.english: Use subtitles at first, then gradually remove them.
sentences.korean: 팟캐스트, 유튜브, TV쇼 배우려는 언어로 된

sentences.english: Don't worry about understanding every word.
Your brain is doing the work even when you feel lost.
sentences.korean: 길을 잃은 것 같을 때도 뇌는 일하고 있어요.

sentences.english: Fifteen minutes a day, every day, will beat a three-hour cramming session once a week.
